programmes.show_description: On the 'Box this time: The Assembly, Genius Game, The Piano, Scam Interceptors, The Americas, Married at First Sight Australia and BBC News (noisy phones).

programmes.show_name: Greg Davies: The Back of My...
programmes.show_description: ...Mum's Head: Greg Davies' acclaimed live comedy. Stung by his mum's suggestion that he's not normal, Greg puts everyone under the microscope in a quest to discover if anyone actually is.
The handcuffed pairs go on a road trip, away from home and family, just the two of them and the iconic, idyllic great British outdoors. Naturally, tempers flare. (Ep5)
It's the final. With a race across Britain and a £100k prize at stake, how do the pairs work together? Do they bond? Or do tensions boil over? And who will be Handcuffed champions? (Ep6)

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien help Helen and her husband Dave to restore a metal garden bench and an old water feature.
Jamie Oliver makes a sublime dukkah roast lamb feast with a fantastic hack to speed up cooking.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
In the prime commuter territory of Hertfordshire, Kirstie and Phil help newly-engaged Liz and Neil in Tring and David and Claire in St Albans.
DIY queen Stacey Solomon helps homeowners salvage their half-built homes. Can she work her magic on Erick and Caroline's extension to bring their 'Japandi' vision back to life? (Ep1/6)
Can Vanessa ruffle feathers with burlesque bravado, a piña colada crumble and face yoga on the penultimate night in Manchester? (Ep4/5)
Sue hosts the week's final visit, at her Victorian townhouse B&B in Penrith, where a marmalade tasting is followed by some bittersweet discussion of payment day.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: Donna fears that she can't deliver the luxury stays she's promised in Italy. Paul and Melissa face challenges restoring a once-prestigious French hotel.
Trish and Ruth love Lanzarote so much that they want to buy a holiday home there, near the popular resort of Puerto del Carmen. Sara Damergi shows them what their £140k can buy.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Danni Menzies returns to Dénia to check in on Alison, Brian and their dogs. Jasmine Harman heads back to sun-kissed Kapparis for a catch-up with Bill and Sandra.
Lincolnshire. First to host is hunting enthusiast Hunni Hindley, who offers up an unconventional menu and a murder mystery-themed party. (Ep1/5)
Lincolnshire. On the second night, Caroline Bevis treats her guests to a 'Roaring 20s' dinner party with a 'swellegant soufflé' starter and 'porkin on the Ritz' for the main. (Ep2/5)
Lincolnshire. Glenn Priestly hosts a rock legends dinner for his guests. But student Richard lowers the tone of the evening. (Ep3/5)
Lincolnshire. Richard Summers-Calvert hopes to win over his guests with an audacious menu of chicken and rice for his starter, main and dessert! (Ep4/5)
Lincolnshire. On the final night, American former barrister Natashka Ingham is determined to show her British rivals how it's done, with a Great American Dream-themed evening. (Ep5/5)
Mark and Loretta look for their dream holiday home near Estepona on Spain's Costa del Sol, for their budget of £200,000. Can Craig Rowe find their perfect place?
In Sussex, Kirstie and Phil look for a period flat in Brighton or Hove and a renovation project with a big wow factor for two architecture buffs.
...Luxurious Cruises: A look behind the scenes aboard some of the world's most opulent cruise ships, where suites cost up to £8k a night and pets are as pampered as their people.
It's double trouble for the Bodleys, as cockapoo twins Milo and Mabel burst into the pen. Can canine and family energy levels match? And Floyd the stray lurcher needs a good feed.
Roofer Simon has fallen two storeys onto concrete, Evelyn's burnt her finger on a cheese stick, and 97-year-old Doris is in resus with a fast heart rate.

programmes.show_name: The Push: Murder on the Cliff
programmes.show_description: True crime documentary. In 2021, a young lawyer fell from the top of Arthur's Seat in Edinburgh. Her husband was soon arrested for murder. As his trial begins, dark secrets emerge. (Ep1/2)

programmes.show_name: The Push: Murder on the Cliff
programmes.show_description: Did young lawyer Fawziyah Javed fall to her death or was she pushed? At her husband's murder trial, the jury are told about a dying declaration before returning their verdict. (Ep2/2)
The Capture, Married at First Sight Australia and Louis Theroux: Inside the Manosphere are some of the shows on this time. And there's also Will and Ralf Should Know Better.
On the 'Box this time: The Assembly, Genius Game, The Piano, Scam Interceptors, The Americas, Married at First Sight Australia and BBC News (noisy phones).
...Experiment: Natalie and Tom are left questioning everything after some steamy intimacy with Josh and Holly, while Chanice and Richardo invite 15 of the residents for an orgy.
Pansexual Sarah can't wait to road test her new body on her first date as a woman. Plumber Jamie's love life has gone down the u-bend, so he's all cisterns go to find a partner.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with Sarah and Ian, who had to house-hunt beyond Balham in south London, and Louise and Matt, who weren't sure about moving north of the Thames.
Stacey helps Jo and Darryl, whose seaside dream in Devon has turned into a nightmare. Can Stacey turn the tide and help them realise their dream of an upside-down living space? (Ep2/6)
In Manchester, final host Danny takes his guests on a culinary journey from Spain to Thailand. Will his night be anything to write home about? And who will bag the £1000? (Ep5/5)
On payment day, a number of B&Bers are certain that their competitors have been doing some tit-for-tat marking. When the dust settles, who'll win the week?

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In Italy, can Donna keep up as holiday reservations fly in? And in France, will unseasonable weather put a dampener on Paul and Melissa's big summer event?
Laura Hamilton helps Jan and her son Jordan find a holiday home near the thriving town of La Duquesa. Are any of Laura's options perfect enough for Jan to put in an offer?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Jean Johansson is back in Portugal with Alison and Mike, who she helped find their dream home. And Ben Hillman catches up with Whithney and Rose in Punta Prima.
Southend-on-Sea. Dog-mad PA Julie-Ann serves up a surfing theme, with two fish dishes and a surf shack in her garden. There's a shock when glamorous guest Carol reveals her age. (Ep1/5)
Southend-on-Sea. Glamorous grandmother Carol Dolman puts on a dazzling display, on a night featuring saucy discoveries, flirty tango lessons and a wig reveal. (Ep2/5)
Southend-on-Sea. Science teacher and self-confessed geek Damian experiments with a spicy menu and a chemistry demo. But Scott brands his main the worst dish he's ever eaten... (Ep3/5)
Southend-on-Sea. Bartender and self-confessed 'airhead' Steph Barrett hosts a 1940s fancy dress night. But she has to call on her nan to bail her out with a pastry problem. (Ep4/5)
Southend-on-Sea. International DJ Scott Langley hosts with a risky menu and an 'outrageous' dress code. But how will his pigeon main go down with his guests? (Ep5/5)
Jo and Graham look for their dream holiday home in Mar Menor, Europe's largest coastal lagoon, on Spain's Costa Blanca. Jasmine Harman shows them impressive properties for their £70k.
Kirstie and Phil catch up with two couples who wanted to buy close to the English Riviera in Torbay: newly-weds Sam and Jamie, and Geoff and Sharon and their family.
Keith and Kate return to the Scottish seaside town of Dunoon, where Kate has fond memories and family ties. But making a Victorian house work as a modern home proves challenging.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: Lexie and Ed's four-bed, one-bath Nottingham home is too small for their family of six. Ed wants to move but Lexie loves the location. Who can help, Kirstie or Phil?
At Queen's Medical Centre in Nottingham, staff treat 16-year-old Kaeden, who's been rushed in after developing a fever, and woodworker Paul, who has partially amputated his thumb.
Ronalds has sliced through his ankle at work. Owen's put his shoulder out at a music festival. Jeremy's bike crash leads to a shocking discovery.
The Goggleboxers hit the couch for more of the biggest and best TV. What do they think this time?
